steps.title: Hear It In Music
steps.instruction: The 4:3 polyrhythm is found in classical hemiola passages, jazz comping, and progressive rock. It creates a sophisticated rhythmic tension.
steps.secondaryText: This pattern is more complex than 3:2, but the learning process is the same.

steps.title: Feel The Shape
steps.instruction: Watch the radial visualizer. Layer A (indigo) plays 4 evenly-spaced beats while Layer B (orange) plays 3. Notice the unique shape this creates.
steps.secondaryText: Observe how the beats interlock differently from 3:2.

steps.title: Learn The Mnemonic
steps.instruction: Say this phrase in rhythm to internalize the 4:3 pattern:
steps.secondaryText: Each syllable aligns with a specific beat in the polyrhythm.

steps.title: Sing The 4
steps.instruction: Listen to just Layer B (the 3-beat pulse). Try singing or tapping the 4-beat pattern on top. Feel how the 4 fits against the 3.
steps.secondaryText: When you feel confident, tap 'I did it!' to move on.

steps.title: Walk + Clap
steps.instruction: Walk in place to the 3-beat pulse (your feet). Clap the 4-beat pattern with your hands. Let your body feel both layers simultaneously.
steps.secondaryText: Keep going for 60 seconds. The timer will count down.

steps.title: Both Hands
steps.instruction: Tap the left zone for Layer A (4 beats) and the right zone for Layer B (3 beats). Try to stay in sync with the audio.
steps.secondaryText: Focus on feeling the groove, not perfection.

steps.title: Disappearing Beat
steps.instruction: The audio will gradually fade away. Can you keep the rhythm going in your head and body?
steps.secondaryText: This is the ultimate test of internalization.
